summary: Fiery Scarlet‑Touch‑Fire and epic Deal dominate this '84 set
blurb: The concert is highlighted by a scorching Scarlet > Touch > Fire sequence and an eleven‑minute, soaring Deal. Jerry’s lead guitar burns while Phil provides a solid bass foundation, and Brent’s keyboards add depth to the transitions. The audience recording captures the high‑energy vibe of the entire show.
review: On July 3, 1984 at Kansas City’s Starlight Theater the Dead delivered one of their most electrifying midsummer outings. From the opening “Alabama Getaway” into a rousing “The Promised Land,” the first set built relentless momentum that culminated in a blistering “Deal” – an eleven‑minute tour de force that left the crowd buzzing. The second set kicked off with a rare and unforgettable “Scarlet Begonias → Touch of Grey → Fire On The Mountain” segue, a sequence reviewers called “tight,” “emotional,” and “central to the show’s high point.” From there the band rode a wave of improvisation through “Jam,” “Space,” and “The Wheel,” before closing with a spirited “Throwing Stones” and a classic “Not Fade Away.” Throughout, Jerry Garcia was “shredding” and “fuzzed out,” especially on the Scarlet‑Touch‑Fire stretch, while Phil Lesh “dropped bombs” that kept the low end rock solid. Brent Mydland’s keyboards painted trippy textures that lifted the transitions, even if a few reviewers noted he sounded a touch tired later in the night. Bob Weir contributed strong vocals and his trademark humor on “Throwing Stones,” and Bill Kreutzmann’s drumming drove the pre‑drums segment with tight, energetic precision. The audience recording (gd84-07-03.aud.willy.14909.sbeok.shnf) captures this energy beautifully – clear, airy three‑dimensional soundstage with well‑balanced mix, making it the definitive listening source for this performance.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: Strong positive sentiment across six of seven recordings; multiple reviewers praise the Scarlet>Touch>Fire sequence, epic Deal, and overall high‑energy performance. No consistent negative comments were noted, yielding a robust +1.5 adjustment.

best_recording.reason: Excellent audience capture with clear, airy three‑dimensional soundstage and balanced mix that highlights the band's high‑energy performance.

key_highlights: Scarlet Begonias → Touch of Grey → Fire On The Mountain segue
Epic eleven‑minute Deal that drives the set forward
High‑energy first set culminating in Alabama Getaway > The Promised Land
Tight pre‑drums segment and vibrant Throwing Stones

band_performance.Jerry: Garcia was on fire, delivering scorching solos on Scarlet, Fire, Deal and Alabama; his lead work was described as shredding and fuzzed out.
band_performance.Phil: Lesh laid down a solid, bomb‑dropping bass foundation that was well‑balanced in the mix and especially tight during the pre‑drums segment.
band_performance.Bob: Weir provided strong vocals on My Brother Esau and added humor on Throwing Stones; his guitar work complemented the high‑energy flow.
band_performance.Brent: Mydland's keyboards were emotional, trippy, and central to the Scarlet > Touch > Fire transition, adding depth despite a hint of fatigue later in the show.
band_performance.Billy: Kreutzmann drove the pre‑drums segment with tight, energetic playing that propelled the band through their peak moments.
